case_title: JOHN MOORE VS. L.D.F INDUSTRIES, INC. ET AL
cause_of_action: OTHER NON EXEMPT COMPLAINTS (COMPLAINT FOR CIVIL PENALTIES AND INJUNCTIVE RELIEF)

docket_entries.description: NOTICE TO PLAINTIFF

DISCOVERY 302, PLAINTIFF JOHN MOORE'S MOTION TO COMPEL RESPONSES TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES AND REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION FROM DEFENDANT L.D.F. INDUSTRIES, INC. AND REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IS CONTINUED TO MAY 30 2017 AT 9:00AM DEPT. 302. JUDGE PRO TEM: DIANNE PEEBLES; CLERK: JOHNNY SENGMANY; NOT REPORTED.=302/JPT.

LAW AND MOTION 302, MOTION TO COMPEL RESPONSES TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES AND REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION FROM DEFENDANT L.D.F. INDUSTRIES, INC. AND REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IS DENIED. LDF HAS NOT APPEARED IN THIS LAWSUIT AND SERVICE OF THE DISCOVERY ON ITS PURPORTED COUNSEL IS NOT SUFFICIENT UNLESS THERE IS STIPULATION TO THAT EFFECT. MR. MOORE'S REMEDY IS TAKE THE DEFAULT OF LDF AND SEEK A DEFAULT JUDGMENT. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR	711, CKAREN@SFTC.ORG
PROOF OF SERVICE (TRANSACTION ID # 61189055) FILED BY PLAINTIFF MOORE, JOHN
MINI MINUTES FOR NOV-21-2017 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

NO OPPOSITION FILED AND THE MOTION COMPLIES WITH THE HEALTH AND SAFETY CODE. THE COURT WILL SIGN THE PROPOSED ORDER AND JUDGMENT AFTER HEARING. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER; CLERK: G. calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-28-2017 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Approve Proposition 65 Settlement And Conset Judgment As To Defendant L.D.F. Industries, Inc.
Off Calendar Nov-21-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Christopher Tuttle, Esq. of The Chanler Group, Tel. 510.848.8880, for plaintiff and moving party;
No other appearance.

RULING: There being no objection, the Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF JOHN MOORE'S Motion To Approve Proposition 65 Settlement And Consent Judgment As To Defendant L.D.F. Industries, Inc. is GRANTED. No opposition filed and the motion complies with the Health and Safety Code. The court will sign the proposed order and judgment after hearing. Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: G. Gonzales; Reporter: Reyes F. Hunter, CSR#6576, e-mail: rhunter@sftc.org.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-02-2017 Continued To Jan-17-18 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jun-30-2017 Continued To Oct-18-17 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Motion To Compel Responses To Special Interrogatories And Requests For Production From Defendant L.D.F. Industries, Inc. And Request For Sanctions
Off Calendar Jun-13-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows: Motion To Compel Responses To Special Interrogatories And Requests For Production From Defendant L.D.F. Industries, Inc. And Request For Sanctions is DENIED. LDF has not appeared in this lawsuit and service of the discovery on its purported counsel is not sufficient unless there is stipulation to that effect. Mr. Moore's remedy is take the default of LDF and seek a default judgment. Prevailing party to submit order in compliance with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR 711, ckaren@sftc.org =(302/hk)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar May-19-2017 Continued To Jul-19-17 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Motion To Compel Responses To Special Interrogatories And Requests For Production From Defendant L.D.F. Industries, Inc. And Request For Sanctions
Off Calendar May-30-2017 Continued To Jun-13-17 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Christopher F. Tuttle, of The Chanler Group, Tel. 510.848.8880, for plaintiffs John Moore;
No other appearances.

Ruling: The matter was discussed. Plaintiff's counsel does not stipulate to the matter being heard by the Judge Pro Tem. Motion To Compel Responses To Special Interrogatories And Requests For Production From Defendant L.D.F. Industries, Inc. And Request For Sanctions is continued to June 13, 2017, at 9:30 a.m., in Department 302, Law & Motion. Judge Pro Tem to prepare report and recommendation. Judge Pro Tem: Roger Mead; Clerk: Felicia Green; Not reported; Dept. 302.
